title: Logical dependency DAG for RiemannHypothesisViaWarpedStationarity.tex
description: Machine-checkable encoding of the lemma chain in the standalone paper 'Zeros of ζ on the Critical Line via the Omer–Torresani Unitary Warping of the Hardy Z-Function' culminating in Theorem thm:main (Riemann Hypothesis). Each node declares its direct dependencies. The DAG is acyclic; topological sort yields a valid proof order. Every non-axiom node must be derivable from its predecessors using only the cited analytic inputs.

AX-Digamma.name: Digamma series and its imaginary part
AX-Digamma.statement: ψ'(s) = ∑_{k≥0}(s+k)^{-2} for s ∉ {0,-1,-2,...}; in particular Im ψ'(¼+it/2) < 0 for t > 0 so ϑ''(t) = −¼ Im ψ'(¼+it/2) > 0 for t > 0

AX-Olver.name: Olver Stirling expansion for ϑ and ϑ'
AX-Olver.statement: ϑ(t) = (t/2)log(t/(2π)) − t/2 + O(1) as t→+∞; ϑ'(t) = ½log(t/(2π)) + O(t^{-2}); in particular ϑ'(2πn²) = log n + O(n^{-4})
AX-Olver.source: Olver, Asymptotics and Special Functions, Ch. 8 §4.3

AX-IFT.name: Inverse function theorem and smoothness of ϑ
AX-IFT.statement: ϑ ∈ C^∞(ℝ) (as imaginary part of log Γ on the real line); a C^∞ bijection with nowhere-vanishing derivative has C^∞ inverse with (Θ^{-1})'(u) = 1/Θ'(Θ^{-1}(u))
AX-IFT.source: Rudin, Principles of Mathematical Analysis, Thm. 9.24

AX-Zreal.name: Z is real on ℝ
AX-Zreal.statement: Z(t) = e^{iϑ(t)}ζ(½+it) is real-valued for t ∈ ℝ; Z(−t) = Z(t)

AX-FE.name: Functional equation of ζ on the critical line
AX-FE.statement: ζ(½−it) = e^{2iϑ(t)} ζ(½+it) for t ∈ ℝ; |e^{iϑ(t)}| = 1 so ζ(½+it₀) = 0 ⇔ Z(t₀) = 0 with matching multiplicities
AX-FE.source: Riemann 1859; Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. 2

AX-HL.name: Hardy–Littlewood second moment and Ingham shifted moment
AX-HL.statement: ∫₀^T |ζ(½+it)|² dt = T log T + (2γ−1−log 2π)T + O(T^{1/2} log T); shifted second moment ∫₀^T ζ(½+it) conj(ζ(½+i(t+η))) dt has an asymptotic main term depending only on η (Ingham 1926)
AX-HL.source: Hardy–Littlewood 1918; Ingham 1926; Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. VII

AX-RS.name: Riemann–Siegel formula
AX-RS.source: Edwards, Riemann's Zeta Function, §7.4 Theorem 7.1; Gabcke Dissertation Göttingen 1979

AX-argR.name: Phase derivative of the Riemann–Siegel remainder
AX-argR.statement: (d/dt) arg R(t) = O(t^{-1/2}) from the Riemann–Siegel contour integral representation
AX-argR.source: Gabcke Dissertation 1979; Berry, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 450 (1995)

AX-Bochner.name: Bochner's theorem
AX-Bochner.statement: A continuous positive-definite function on ℝ is the Fourier transform of a finite non-negative Borel measure on ℝ

AX-WK.name: Wiener–Khinchin identification of spectral measure
AX-WK.statement: For a time-average WSS function with covariance c(η) = ∫ e^{iηξ} dμ(ξ), μ is the spectral measure; its absolutely continuous part has Radon–Nikodym derivative equal to the power spectral density S(ξ) = lim 2π|K_T(ξ)|²/T (a function, not a measure) where the limit exists
AX-WK.source: Wiener 1930; Khinchin 1934; Doob, Stochastic Processes, Ch. X §3

AX-PW-WSS.name: Paley–Wiener for WSS functions with compactly supported spectral measure
AX-PW-WSS.statement: If a locally square-integrable function f : ℝ → ℂ is time-average wide-sense stationary with covariance c_f(η) = ∫ e^{iηξ} dμ(ξ) and supp μ ⊆ [−σ,σ], then f admits an entire extension of exponential type ≤ σ satisfying |f(z)| ≤ ‖μ‖^{1/2} e^{σ|Im z|} on ℂ
AX-PW-WSS.source: Yaglom, Correlation Theory of Stationary and Related Random Functions, Vol. I, Thm. 4.7.3; Bochner–Phillips, Ann. Math. 43 (1942)

AX-Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer Laguerre–Pólya criterion
AX-Akhiezer.statement: A real entire function F of exponential type ≤ τ whose autocorrelation measure (Fourier transform of |F|² as a tempered distribution) is a non-negative finite Borel measure supported in [−2τ,2τ] belongs to the Laguerre–Pólya class; all complex zeros of F are real
AX-Akhiezer.source: Akhiezer, Theory of Approximation, §V.4; Levin, Lectures on Entire Functions, Lecture 16 Thm. 3

lem:Thetabij.name: Monotone phase shift Θ is a C^∞ bijection
lem:Thetabij.statement: For every c > c_* := −ϑ'(0), Θ(t) := ϑ(t) + ct satisfies Θ'(t) > 0 on ℝ and Θ : ℝ → ℝ is a C^∞ bijection with C^∞ inverse

lem:unitary.name: Unitarity and composition of the Omer–Torresani operator
lem:unitary.statement: [𝒟_γ x](t) := √(γ'(t)) x(γ(t)) preserves L² norm on ℝ for any C¹ diffeomorphism γ with γ'>0; composition 𝒟_γ 𝒟_φ = 𝒟_{φ∘γ}, inverse (𝒟_γ)^{-1} = 𝒟_{γ^{-1}}

lem:Yreal.name: Y is real on ℝ
lem:Yreal.statement: Y(u) ∈ ℝ for every u ∈ ℝ

lem:HL.name: Hardy–Littlewood–Ingham second moment (paper statement)
lem:HL.statement: ∫₀^T |ζ(½+it)|² dt = T log T + (2γ−1−log 2π)T + O(T^{1/2} log T); shifted moment given by Ingham's theorem

thm:Ywss.name: Y is wide-sense stationary in the time-average sense
thm:Ywss.statement: c_Y(η) := lim_{U→∞} U^{-1} ∫₀^U Y(u) conj(Y(u+η)) du exists for every η ∈ ℝ and depends only on η

cor:Cramer.name: Cramér spectral representation of c_Y
cor:Cramer.statement: There exists a finite non-negative Borel measure μ_Y on ℝ with c_Y(η) = ∫ e^{iηξ} dμ_Y(ξ)

thm:bandlim.name: Band-limitedness of the windowed Fourier transform
thm:bandlim.statement: For every admissible c and every μ ∈ ℝ with |μ| > 1, S(μ) = lim 2π|K_T(μ)|²/Θ(T) = 0

thm:PW.name: Paley–Wiener entire extension of Y
thm:PW.statement: Y extends to an entire function of exponential type ≤ 1 with |Y(z)| ≤ μ_Y(ℝ)^{1/2} e^{|Im z|}

thm:Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer Laguerre–Pólya criterion (paper statement)
thm:Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function of exponential type ≤ τ whose autocorrelation measure (Fourier transform of |F|² as a tempered distribution) is non-negative and supported in [−2τ,2τ] lies in the Laguerre–Pólya class; all complex zeros are real

thm:LP.name: Reality of zeros of Y
thm:LP.statement: All zeros of the entire extension of Y are real

thm:zero-bij.name: Zero-set bijection Z ↔ Y via unitary warping
thm:zero-bij.statement: t₀ ∈ ℝ is a zero of Z of order m if and only if Θ(t₀) ∈ ℝ is a zero of Y of order m

cor:Zreal.name: Reality of zeros of Z
cor:Zreal.statement: Every zero of Z in ℂ lies on ℝ

thm:main.name: Zeros of ζ on the critical line (Riemann Hypothesis)
thm:main.statement: For every t₀ ∈ ℝ, ζ(½+it₀) = 0 iff Y(Θ(t₀)) = 0 with equal multiplicities; consequently every nontrivial zero of ζ satisfies Re ρ = ½

notes: Every node either is an axiom (kind=axiom, deps=[]) or cites its direct dependencies by ID.
Target is thm:main (RH on the critical line).

The chain: lem:Thetabij (Θ bijection) + lem:unitary (𝒟_γ unitarity) → def:Y → lem:Yreal; separately def:Y + lem:HL → thm:Ywss → cor:Cramer; def:Y + lem:RS → thm:bandlim → cor:supp; cor:Cramer + cor:supp → thm:PW; thm:PW + lem:Yreal + cor:supp + thm:Akhiezer → thm:LP; thm:LP + thm:zero-bij + AX-FE → thm:main.
Axioms: AX-Digamma, AX-Olver, AX-IFT, AX-Zreal, AX-FE, AX-HL, AX-RS, AX-argR, AX-Bochner, AX-WK, AX-PW-WSS, AX-Akhiezer.

The 16-item errata attached to the paper lists the propositions whose written derivation is not complete; every item except Item 1 (𝒟_γ composition-direction notational swap) feeds into a node on the path to thm:main.
